name: Villaverde del Monte from Estépar —
name_original: Villaverde del Monte desde Estépar — circular por Arenillas de Muñó
description: This moderate 33.7-mile touring cycling route from Estépar explores rural Burgos, passing charming villages and Arenillas de Muñó Castle.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Estépar traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including areas with notable elevation changes as indicated by the routes' ascent profiles. The region, located in the province of Burgos, offers a rural setting for cycling. These routes connect various local villages and towns, providing opportunities to experience the area's open spaces. Best touring cycling routes around Estépar The most popular touring cycling route is Villaverde del Monte from Estépar —, a 33.7 miles (54.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route features moderate elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced touring cyclists. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Burgos – View of Hontanas loop from Estépar, a difficult 45.3 miles (72.9 km) path. This route offers significant climbs and panoramic views across the landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Burgos – Burgos Cathedral loop from Tardajos, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Touring cycling around Estépar is defined by its open rural landscapes and varied elevation profiles, offering options for different ability levels. The cycling routes Estépar provide opportunities to explore the region's villages and natural surroundings. The routes in Estépar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Estépar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Estépar?
A: There are nearly 30 touring cycling routes around Estépar, offering a range of distances and difficulties to suit various preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Estépar?
A: The touring cycling routes around Estépar traverse a varied landscape, characterized by open rural settings and areas with notable elevation changes. You'll experience a mix of flat sections and climbs as you explore the region's villages and natural surroundings.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Estépar?
A: Yes, Estépar offers several routes suitable for beginners. There are 3 easy touring cycling routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33726118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos – Burgos Cathedral loop from Tardajos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.4 miles (21.6 km) long and takes about 1 hour 19 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Estépar?
A: For experienced touring cyclists, Estépar offers 17 difficult routes. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40305893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hontanas – Convent of San Antón loop from Estépar&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 61.1 miles (98.3 km) with significant elevation gain, taking approximately 6 hours 24 minutes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Estépar?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Estépar are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burgos – View of Hontanas loop from Estépar&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.3 miles (72.9 km) circular path offering panoramic views.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Estépar?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can explore various points of interest. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4567194" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hontanas&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1014176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Immaculate Conception, Hontanas&lt;/a&gt;, and the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6670690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornillos del Camino&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4617088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Monastery&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5811895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Hierro of Hornillos del Camino&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Estépar?
A: The touring cycling routes in Estépar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the open rural landscapes and the varied elevation profiles that offer options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any places to stay or refresh along the routes?
A: While specific cafes are not detailed for every route, there are huts and cabins in the area that can serve as places to stay or refresh. Options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5017043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Casa de Beli&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5026850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue Juan de Yepes&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4417930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue Fuente Sidres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route in Estépar?
A: One of the longest touring cycling routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of San Antón – View of Hontanas loop from Villanueva de Argaño&lt;/a&gt;, covering 61.8 miles (99.5 km) with significant elevation, making it a challenging ride.

Q: Is Estépar suitable for family-friendly touring cycling?
A: Yes, Estépar offers easy touring cycling routes that can be suitable for families, especially those with some cycling experience. These routes typically have less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a more relaxed experience for all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Estépar?
A: While specific weather details are not provided, generally, spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for cycling in this region of Spain, avoiding the extreme heat of summer or the colder conditions of winter.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through multiple villages?
A: Yes, many routes connect various local villages and towns, allowing you to experience the area's rural charm. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030178878/villaverde-del-monte-from-estepar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaverde del Monte from Estépar —&lt;/a&gt; route takes you through different local communities.
tips: Currently private property used as housing and warehouse. It is a defensive tower built by the Padillas in the 15th century. It is about 18 meters high and is topped by a crown of machicolations.

Rolling through Presencio, visit its main places of interest: the Renaissance-style Church of San Andrés from the 16th century, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest, remains of the wall (arch), the jurisdictional Gothic scroll of the Plaza, with the coat of arms of the House from Austria.

faqs.question: How long does this touring cycling route typically take?
faqs.answer: The route, named "Villaverde del Monte from Estépar — circular by Arenillas de Muñó", is approximately 54.3 km long and typically takes around 3 hours and 35 minutes to complete on a touring bicycle.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. You can expect rolling hills and a mix of surfaces, including rural paved roads and potentially some gravel paths. While greenways in the broader Burgos region tend to have gentle gradients, other rural roads can be varied. It's advisable to be prepared for different surfaces.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead parking lot isn't detailed, as the route starts "from Estépar", it's recommended to look for suitable parking within or on the outskirts of Estépar village. Rural areas generally offer more informal parking options.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll pass through charming rural landscapes and have the opportunity to see historical points of interest. Notable highlights include the Arenillas de Muñó Castle and the settlement of Presencio, offering glimpses into the region's heritage.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, cycling on rural roads and paths in this region is dog-friendly, provided your dog is accustomed to cycling alongside or in a trailer. Always keep your dog under control, especially when passing through villages or near livestock, and be mindful of local regulations regarding leashes.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and paths through the countryside.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder. Summer can be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter cycling is possible but can be affected by colder weather and potentially wet or muddy conditions on unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through villages like Estépar, Villaverde del Monte, and Arenillas de Muñó, where you may find small local establishments such as cafes, bars, or restaurants. For accommodation, it's best to check in the larger villages or nearby towns.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other notable cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of the Ruta de Carlos V. It also utilizes various local paths and streets, including segments of Calle Juan Diez Cordero, Calle Reina Juana, and Camino de los Arenales.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as indicated by its name, "Villaverde del Monte from Estépar — circular by Arenillas de Muñó", this is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point in Estépar.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating and distance of over 54 km, this route might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners. While some sections may be easy, the overall length and varied terrain suggest it's more suitable for cyclists with some experience or good fitness levels.
